title: Product of Array Except Self
description:
Given an integer array `nums`, return an array `answer` such that `answer[i]`
equals the product of all the elements of `nums` **except** `nums[i]`.

Solve it **without using division** and in O(n) time.
constraints: The product of any prefix or suffix fits in a 32-bit integer.

solutions.summary: For each index multiply all the others. Simple but O(n²).

steps.title: Multiply all the other indices
steps.explanation: For each `i`, loop a second index `j`, multiplying every value where `j != i`.

solutions.summary: Sweep left building prefix products, then right multiplying suffixes.

steps.title: Fill prefix products
steps.explanation: Set `res[i]` to the product of everything **before** `i`, carried in a running `prefix`.

code.python:
def product_except_self(nums):
    n = len(nums)
    res = [1] * n
    prefix = 1
    for i in range(n):
        res[i] = prefix
        prefix *= nums[i]
    return res

steps.title: Multiply in the suffix products
steps.explanation: Sweep from the right with a running `suffix`, multiplying it into each `res[i]`.

code.python:
def product_except_self(nums):
    n = len(nums)
    res = [1] * n
    prefix = 1
    for i in range(n):
        res[i] = prefix
        prefix *= nums[i]
    suffix = 1
    for i in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
        res[i] *= suffix
        suffix *= nums[i]
    return res
